title: Got FOMO? Watch This. | June 7, 2025

So the number one thing we do for our
clients here at 42 macro, we help them
identify a position for the market
regime via our global macro risk matrix
that determines the market regime. And
then we filter those signals to retail
investors via KISS. And we filter those
signals to institutional investors via
Dr. Mo. And then everything else we say
is purely designed to help you build the
confidence required to dispassionately
execute our KISS and Dr. Mo signals uh
when it's time to dispassionally execute
them. So and and the best way we figured
out how to do that is to help you
understand the full distribution of
profit. economic outcomes at all times
so that you're comfortable with whatever
the market decides it wants to price in.
You know, it's not our job to tell the
market to price in Goldilocks or
reflation, inflation or deflation. It's
the market's job to deter to to
determine that. It's our job to notice
it and pay attention and do something
differently in our portfolio when the
market decides it wants to do something
differently in terms of what it wants to
price in. That's that's that's our if
you want to remain on the right side of
market risk, you don't have to remain on
the right side of market risk. you can
remain on the wrong side of market risk
indefinitely uh as a retail investor
until your account hits zero as an
institutional investor as someone on the
buy side you stay on the wrong side of
market risk for a little you know more
than a couple of months you might be you
know selling insurance and so this is
why we built our process this is an
institutional macro risk management
process and I dare say dare say it's
highly effective we use our global macro
risk matrix to now cast a market regime
which investors must position for to
avoid FOMO or FOMO fear of missing out
causes investors to high cycle tops and
fear of more losses causes investors to
sell cycle lows. How many people are out
there suffering from intense FOMO right
now? Millions
perhaps. You know why they're in
suffering from intense FOMO? They're
getting waterboarded with tariff talk
all day every day and their general
sense of economic anxiety and policy
uncertainties at an all-time high or as
I we see via the Baker Bloom Davis index
is as high as it's been at the height of
COVID and the height of the global
financial crisis. Now, in my opinion,
this is not the height of CO or the
global financial crisis. People are just
going nuts. And so in my opinion, uh,
take advantage of the fact that they're
going to have to capitulate to the
upside in the coming quarters. It may
not be next week, next month, or even in
the next three months, but over in our
view, over the next 12 months, there's a
large swath of investor consensus that
needs to do something about the FOMO
they're feeling. You know what they're
going to do? They're going to buy. You
want to be, you want to have already
bought before they get their buy. You
want to be in line in front of them. You
don't want to buy after they buy. You
want to buy before they buy. And that's
what KISS and Dr. Mo do it's it's it's
trying to stay at the van help tra it's
trying to keep your portfolio at the
vanguard of what everyone else knows.
I'm not smart enough to know everything.
Uh news
flash. I'm pretty smart but I'm not
smart enough to know everything. You
know who knows more than me? The S&P,
the emerging MSI emerging market index,
the NIK 225, the NASDAQ, the Russell
2000, Shanghai composite and the euro
stocks. implied volatility correlations,
currency volatility, bond market
volatility, uh equity volatility, crude
oil, uh ad commodities, industrial
commodities, silver, bitcoin, Aussie
dollar, US dollar, euro, British pound,
Swiss frank, Chinese yuan, Japanese yen,
gold, 10-year German break evens,
10-year uh German nominals, 10-year
Japanese nominals um uh uh 10 rates,
10-year UK nominal rates, uh the high
yield credit spreads, high yield yields,
investment grade credit spreads,
investment grade yields, one-year US
dollar OIS one year US um euro OIS one
year uh the British pound os 10ear uh
British pound break evens uh 10 year
treasuries two-year treasuries 30-year
treasuries 10 year US break evens 10
year real tip shield and the two 10
curve these markets know way more than I
do because there's other investors
participating in those markets
determining the bam signal and that's
why we use this to determine what the
market regime is. I don't just wake up
and go, "Well, I did some research that
was bullish, so therefore I think the
market regime should be bullish." Or, "I
did some research that was bearish,
therefore the the market should be
bearish." That's not how the world
works. You have to separate what you
think and just accept the market is
smarter than you and is going to do
whatever the heck the market wants to
do. So, it's our job to ride the bull or
get out of the way of the bear. It's not
our job to tell the bull to bull or tell
the bear to bear. It's our job to ride
the bull or get out of the way of the
bear. If you start investing with that
level of humility, your results will be
way better. And the reason we are
systematic investors because it forces
that level of humility upon me and the
things that I communicate to you. And so
what I'm communicating to you today is
that we remain in a Goldilocks market
regime. 22 of these markets. I'm
knocking on the door of 42 of the
world's most important markets and
saying, "Hey, what do you think about
the market? What do you think market?
Hey, what do S&P? What do you think?
10ear Treasury yield? What do you think?
Currency volatility? What do you think?
Imply volatility correlations. What do
you think? And and each one of these,
every single day, every single day, they
have an answer. And if we tally up their
answer based on how they've historically
traded across the four different market
regimes, Goldilocks is risk with the
disinflationary bias. Reflation is risk
with an inflationary bias. Inflation is
risk off with an inflationary bias, and
deflation is risk off with the
disinflationary bias. based on how
they've historically traded. Each of
these individual features in this model
will tell me, hey, there's more of us
that think it's a goldilocks than there
is reflation, inflation, and deflation.
Therefore, we as investors are going to
orient our portfolios with the people
with the with the regime that has the
most love. You know, Kiss and Dr. Mo are
going to reflect being in Goldilocks.
And when that changes, it'll change. And
and then we just have to dispassionately
execute that signal. I don't know how
long we'll be in Goldilocks. I can paint
a very credible picture that we can
remain in Goldilocks between now and
let's call it next
summer based on our paradigm C basis.
But there's also some things that I
think the market might get concerned
about along the way which may create,
you know, a transitory risk off
inflation market regime or transitory
risk offlation market regime. I don't
know. It's not my job to to to tell you,
oh, that's going to happen on August
2nd. It's my job to wake up and do my
job on August 2nd and if it happens on
August 2nd, signal it to you so that you
guys can make the appropriate pivots in
your portfolio to manage risk in order
to remain on the right side of market
risk so that you can compound returns
from a higher net asset value than the
other people who are suffering the draw
down and suffering through the
volatility drag. If you compound if you
stop if you comp you you account should
look like
this. Other people's accounts look like
this. This is stressful as hell. You
should be trying to avoid that. Period.
